channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some Israeli soldiers traveling abroad are targeted for alleged war crimes in Gaza
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An Israeli army reservist's dream vacation in Brazil ended abruptly last month over an accusation that he committed war crimes in the Gaza Strip. Yuval Vagdani woke up on Jan.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The iconic ‘T’ stands for twisted in Tory Burch’s new collection at New York Fashion Week
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tory Burch not only put her own twist on American sportswear in her new fall/winter collection at New York Fashion Week — she made it “twist-ed,” with exaggerated shapes and details, a splashy backdrop and memorable music.
item.dc:creator: BROOKE LEFFERTS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Middle East latest: Hamas brushes off Trump and insists all parties must be committed to ceasefire
item.description: Hamas has brushed off President Donald Trump’s threat that “all hell” will break out if it does not release the remaining Israeli hostages held in the Gaza Strip by Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about proposals to ban abortion pills and punish women who seek abortion
item.description: Lawmakers in some states are pushing measures to crack down on abortion pills or penalize women who obtain abortions.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: They have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle. Meet, for example, GCHG CH Calicops Sassafras Gonnakikurass. “She’s a saucy girl. Her name says it all,” Fred Ortiz said as he groomed the Brussels griffon to compete Monday at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US judge keeps Trump plan to push out federal workers on hold
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's plan to downsize the federal workforce remains on hold after a courtroom hearing on Monday afternoon. It's the latest example of how the Republican president's ambitious plans have become ensnared in the judicial system. U.S. District Judge George O’Toole Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Election official in Arizona’s largest county signals a shift away from combating disinformation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — One of the top election officials in Arizona's largest county, which has been roiled in recent years by conspiracy theories and intimidation of election workers, is moving away from the office's previous efforts to combat misinformation.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Labor unions sue to block DOGE access to sensitive information at US agencies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of labor unions filed a lawsuit Monday asking a federal court to stop Elon Musk’s team from accessing private data at the Education Department, the Treasury Department and the Office of Personnel Management.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Justice Department official orders prosecutors to drop charges against New York Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: The Justice Department has ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against New York Mayor Eric Adams and halt the ongoing investigation.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Alanna Durkin Richer And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing 1 person, authorities say
item.description: Authorities say one person has been killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FEMA says it's halting payments for migrant housing in New York after Musk blasts money for hotels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The acting head of the federal agency responsible for responding to disasters said Monday that he’s suspending payments sent to New York City to house migrants and that staff who made them will be held accountable, after Elon Musk blasted the transactions on his social media platfo
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Department of Homeland Security looking to deputize IRS agents for immigration enforcement
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem sent a letter to U.S. Treasury secretary Scott Bessent, asking that IRS agents be provided to assist with immigration and enforcement efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested in Virginia for allegedly trespassing at three schools
item.description: Zachary Cruz, the brother of the Parkland shooter Nikolas Cruz was arrested last week for allegedly trespassing at three Virginia schools.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Person killed in Union Station shooting during rush hour
item.description: WASHINGTON (DC News Now) -- A person was shot and killed during rush hour Monday afternoon at D.C.'s Union Station. The United States Capitol Police (USCP) said officers responded to Union Station at around 4:40 p.m., where they found one person who had been shot.
item.dc:creator: Gabby Allen, NewsNation

item.title: Confusion after emergency alert sent to phones in San Francisco
item.description: (KRON) -- Some cell phone users in San Francisco received an emergency alert Monday afternoon. Phones sounded the emergency alert tone after a notification said that an endangered adult was missing.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Virginia court overturns felony convictions against Tiger King star 'Doc' Antle
item.description: "Doc" Antle was convicted in June 2023 on two counts each of purchasing an endangered species and conspiracy to sell or purchase an endangered species.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, NewsNation

item.title: American tourists attacked by shark at luxe Bahamas resort area
item.description: Police are investigating after two female American tourists were apparently involved in a shark attack at a popular Bahamas resort over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hoarder arrested for extreme animal cruelty, as authorities discover 10 dead pets, rescue many more: police
item.description: A Long Island woman was arrested and charged with animal cruelty and other charges after a search warrant on her residence revealed unlivable conditions for dozens of animals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Palestinians will have no right of return to Gaza under US proposal
item.description: No, they wouldn’t,” Trump declared. “In other words, I'm talking about building a permanent place for them.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutor says Salman Rushdie was too stunned to react when a man started to stab him
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Salman Rushdie was so stunned when a masked man started to stab him on a stage in western New York that the author didn't even try to fight back, a prosecutor said Monday during opening statements in the suspect's attempted murder trial.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles family featured in Super Bowl commercial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Taco Bell marked its return to the Super Bowl with a commercial that featured nearly 400 fans, including a couple from Los Angeles and their newborn baby.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Illegal alien charged in sandwich shop owner's murder caught and released into US under Biden: ICE
item.description: An illegal alien from the Dominican Republic has been charged with the home invasion murder of a Massachusetts sandwich shop owner named Ilias "Louie" Mavros.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catalytic converters stolen from Illinois district's school buses, forcing students to stay home
item.description: A suburban Illinois school district was forced to hold an emergency e-learning day after the catalytic converters were stolen from "over 80%" of its buses.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jon Batiste's reimagined Super Bowl anthem highlighted wife's painting and set stage for new music
item.description: Jon Batiste transformed the Super Bowl stage into a symphony of creativity, debuting new music, reimagining “The Star-Spangled Banner" and shining a global spotlight on his wife's creative vision.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is the Super Bowl halftime singer lip syncing? Check for these signs
item.description: Casual fans will be watching the theatrics, choreography and guest appearances during Kendrick Lamar and SZA's Super Bowl halftime show. Sleuths will be watching their throats.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says 25 percent tariffs on steel and aluminum coming Monday
item.description: Trump said that the steel and aluminum tariffs would impact “everybody” when asked what countries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump official orders consumer protection agency to stop work
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canada beats US 3-1 in Game 5 to win the Rivalry Series women's hockey showcase
item.description: Jennifer Gardiner broke a tie with 6:44 left and Canada beat the United States 3-1 on Saturday night to win the Rivalry Series women's hockey showcase for the third straight time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James will sit out the Lakers' game against Indiana to rest his sore left ankle
item.description: LeBron James is sitting out the Los Angeles Lakers ' game against Indiana on Saturday to rest his sore left ankle.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, ABC 4
